title: The Inner Way
text: The soul that truly seeks God must enter into itself and find Him in the deepest center of the heart. All the noise of the world must be silenced, all our outward activities must be brought under the governance of the Spirit. God does not dwell in the marketplace of our thoughts but in the sanctuary of our surrendered will. Let the soul be still and know that He is God.

title: Abandonment to Divine Providence
text: Nothing happens to the soul that loves God by chance. Every trial, every sorrow, every moment of darkness is permitted by the hand of infinite love. The soul must not resist but abandon itself entirely to the will of God, as clay in the hands of the potter. This holy abandonment is the shortest path to union with God.

title: Prayer of the Heart
text: True prayer is not the multiplication of words but the stretching of the heart toward God. A single sigh of love is worth more than a thousand recited prayers spoken without attention. God looks not at the eloquence of our speech but at the disposition of our heart. Let us offer Him the silence of our love.

title: The Cross is the Path to Joy
text: Do not be surprised that the way to joy passes through the cross. Christ Himself learned obedience through suffering, and we who are His disciples must walk the same road. The cross is not the enemy of joy but its instrument. In the very place where we feel most abandoned, God is most intimately present.

title: Simplicity of Faith
text: The soul must come to God as a little child comes to its mother -- without reasoning, without demanding proofs, simply trusting. The learned mind often finds faith difficult because it seeks to comprehend what can only be loved. God is known by the heart far more than by the understanding.
